publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2024-07-27 00:21:07 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.langmaterial: Materials are primarily in English. Some materials are in Hebrew, Arabic, French, German, and Russian. Materials in languages other than English are indicated at the file level.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Collection contains teaching materials, notes, and research relating to the career of David Powers. Also includes departmental records from his time as chair of the Department of Near Eastern Studies at Cornell University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: David S. Powers received his Ph.D. from Princeton in 1979 and began teaching at Cornell in the same year. He currently holds positions as a Professor in the Department of Near Eastern, an Adjunct Professor at the Cornell Law School. His courses deal with Islamic civilization, Islamic history and law, and classical Arabic texts, and his research focuses on the emergence of Islam and Islamic legal history. He is founding editor of the journal Islamic Law and Society.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David S. Powers papers, #14-30-4531. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
